channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration halts legal aid for migrant children, leaving some to navigate courts alone
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Children so young their feet can't touch the floor when they sit in courtroom chairs have been left without representation in immigration court because of Trump administration cuts, according to organizations that help these kids navigate the legal system when they arrive in the U.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politicians demand cuts to 9/11 health program be reversed
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The 9/11 health program is taking a heavy hit from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. Twenty percent of the program's staff were just fired or took buyouts. Already strained workers will now be slower in processing the sick, with far greater wait times and delays.
item.dc:creator: John Muller, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of criminal case against NYC mayor
item.description: The Justice Department’s second-in-command demanded that a judge dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams. The mayor, who faces conspiracy, wire fraud and bribery charges in a five-count indictment, declared, “I have not committed a crime.” Manhattan federal Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas mental health licensing board gives initial OK to remove training requirement associated with DEI
item.description: Officials say the motive for the change to the rule requiring mental health providers to receive cultural diversity or competency training for license renewals has been misunderstood.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Stephen Simpson, KPRC 2

item.title: Surprising new poll numbers released on Trump's performance so far in the White House
item.description: Three new national polls released indicate President Donald Trump's approval ratings are edging down slightly since taking over in the White House one month ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge homes in on DOGE staffer duties in privacy lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday looked to pinpoint the exact duties of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers during a hearing over whether to cut off their unfettered access to troves of sensitive personal data. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Eli Crane stunned at 'insane' sum in taxpayer funds used for transgender animal studies
item.description: Congressional lawmakers were told earlier this month about the staggering price tag in taxpayer money used to conduct studies on animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmaker who led state to require ethnic studies in schools decries effort to undo it
item.description: State Rep. Samantha Sencer-Mura, DFL-Minneapolis, authored the bill that set ethnic studies standards a few years ago. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday in response to Republican Rep. Ron Kresha’s legislation.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: HUD offices became as vacant as a ‘Spirit Halloween’ store under Biden: admin sources
item.description: D.C.'s Housing and Urban Development headquarters is among the federal buildings that turned into empty "Spirit Halloween" stores under the Biden administration, Fox Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No decision from judge on DOJ request to drop case against Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The federal judge considering the Justice Department's request to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams said Wednesday that he won't rule immediately and will take time to review all materials. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: The new head of the Social Security Administration says deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some of the 400 jobs that were cut at the FAA helped support air safety, a union says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has said no one at the Federal Aviation Administration with a “critical safety” position has been fired as it cuts the federal workforce, but some FAA jobs that were eliminated had direct roles in supporting safety inspectors and airport oper
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet Dale E. Ho, the second-year judge weighing the future of NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer asked, top officials in President Donald Trump's Justice Department agreed, and seven prosecutors resigned in protest. Now, it’s up to Dale E. Ho, a second-year federal judge in Manhattan, to decide if Adams' corruption case goes away.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota business leader among thousands of federal workers axed in Trump cuts
item.description: Thousands of probationary employees who have not been in their jobs long enough to qualify for certain protections have been terminated by federal agencies around the country, including in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Kelly Loeffler to lead Small Business Administration
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed former Sen. Kelly Loeffler (R-Ga.) to lead the Small Business Administration (SBA), keeping up a frantic pace to cement President Trump's team in place. Senators voted 52-46 on Loeffler's nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge to weigh arguments for dropping NYC Mayor Eric Adams' charges
item.description: Adams pleaded not guilty in September to allegations that he accepted at least $100K in illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: National Science Foundation fires roughly 10% of its workforce
item.description: NSF fired 168 employees, leaving the agency less equipped to fund a wide range of scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Trump 'lives' in Russian 'disinformation space': Zelenskyy
item.description: Zelenskyy's claim follows Trump's news conference Tuesday evening, where he criticized the Ukrainian leader.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Americans' confidence in air travel safety dips slightly after Washington plane crash: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans' confidence in air travel and the federal agencies tasked with maintaining air safety has slipped a little from last year, following a recent crash in Washington, according to a new poll, but most still believe air transportation is generally safe.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ and NYC mayor face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who is signaling that he’s unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop the mayor's corruption charges weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Should Hochul remove Adams? Former NY Gov. Paterson weighs in
item.description: Former New York Gov. David Paterson joins NewsNation's "CUOMO" to discuss the political turmoil surrounding NYC Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order at Mar-a-Lago expanding IVF access
item.description: "The Order directs policy recommendations to protect IVF access and aggressively reduce out-of-pocket and health plan costs for such treatments," the White House press secretary wrote.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: "I have decided it is time to start the process of identifying my successor and of preparing the Postal Service for this change,” DeJoy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Health agencies lose staff members in key areas as Trump firings set in
item.description: As the dust settles from the first wave of firings at health agencies, here's how many people got cut and the impact of the roles that were lost.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
